## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On October 29, 1929, the incredibly massive, heavily catastrophic, and globally devastating stock market crash universally known as "Black Tuesday" violently struck the massive New York Stock Exchange. Following the initial panic of Black Thursday, the absolute, unprecedented panic selling aggressively plummeted the absolute market value, instantly wiping out billions of dollars in massive global wealth. This incredibly massive, profound financial collapse fundamentally and aggressively initiated the absolutely devastating, massive decade-long Great Depression, heavily altering the absolute economic trajectory of the entire 20th-century world.

On October 29, 1969, an incredibly massive, absolutely foundational, and historically profound technological transmission successfully and aggressively occurred in California. Researchers under the massive ARPANET project sent the absolute, very first message over a massive computer network from UCLA to the Stanford Research Institute. Although the massive system aggressively crashed after the first two letters ("LO"), this incredibly massive, highly complex engineering triumph absolutely laid the massive, fundamental architectural bedrock for the entire modern internet.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On October 29, 1920, the incredibly massive, absolutely historic, and universally revered foundational institution of Indian Islamic education, the Jamia Millia Islamia, was officially and aggressively founded in Aligarh (later massively shifted to New Delhi). Heavily championed by incredibly massive nationalist leaders including Dr. Zakir Husain and Maulana Mahmud Hasan, the massive university was fiercely designed to completely boycott the deeply oppressive British colonial educational system and heavily fuse massive modern education with absolute, profound Islamic heritage.

He achieved absolute, historic sporting immortality by becoming the very first Indian boxer to win a massive Olympic medal, aggressively securing bronze at the 2008 Beijing Olympics. His massive, incredibly powerful fighting style and absolute, unwavering dedication heavily revolutionized the massive global perception of Indian boxing and fiercely inspired a massive new generation of Indian pugilists.
